name: Basic Curry Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2-inch piece root Ginger peeled and sliced
2 Fleshy green chiles of your choice, seeded and chopped
4 tbsp Plain passata (smooth, thick, sieved tomatoes)
recipeInstructions:
This is the basis for many of the restaurant-style curries you'll find. The curry recipes use half or two-thirds of the amount this recipe gives. If you have leftovers, you could use them in a vegetable curry or freeze them. It will keep well in the freezer but only for a few weeks. Remember to wrap it up well; your ice-cream may take on a strange taste.
1. Heat the oil in a heavy pan, then add the chopped onion and stir for a few minutes with the heat on high.
2. Add the ginger, garlic, green chile, turmeric, cumin, and coriander. Stir for 30 seconds, then reduce the heat to very low.
3. Cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally to ensure nothing browns or burns.
4. Turn off the heat and let cool slightly. Add 4 fl oz cold water to a blender; add the contents of the pan and blend until very smooth.
5. Transfer the puréed mixture back into the pan, add salt, then cook for 30 minutes over low heat, stirring occasionally to prevent it from catching on the pan. If needed, add a little hot water to keep it moist.
